root_cause.text: The `readStored` method in `ZipArchiveInputStream` silently returned `-1` upon encountering a truncated entry without updating its internal buffer limits, allowing subsequent reads to incorrectly return data instead of EOF, which caused infinite loops in consumers like `InputStreamReader`.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `ZipArchiveInputStream.java` modifies the `readStored` method to set `buf.limit(0)` and throw an `IOException("Truncated ZIP file")` instead of just returning `-1` when `in.read()` returns EOF. Additionally, the `changes.xml` specifically states that `read` would silently return `-1` on corrupted entries and then return `> 0` after hitting the end.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `readStored` method in `ZipArchiveInputStream` silently returned `-1` upon encountering a truncated entry without updating its internal buffer limits, allowing subsequent reads to incorrectly return data instead of EOF, which caused infinite loops in consumers like `InputStreamReader`.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `ZipArchiveInputStream.java` modifies the `readStored` method to set `buf.limit(0)` and throw an `IOException("Truncated ZIP file")` instead of just returning `-1` when `in.read()` returns EOF. Additionally, the `changes.xml` specifically states that `read` would silently return `-1` on corrupted entries and then return `> 0` after hitting the end.

intent_record.intent_summary: Document the bug fix for ZipArchiveInputStream in the release notes.
intent_record.what: Added an action tag to the release notes documenting a fix for COMPRESS-463.
intent_record.how: Updates the changelog documentation to inform users about the resolution of a bug in ZipArchiveInputStream.

intent_record.intent_summary: Throw an IOException on EOF to properly handle and signal truncated ZIP files.
intent_record.what: Replaced returning -1 with throwing an IOException and setting the buffer limit to 0 when the input stream reaches EOF unexpectedly.
intent_record.how: Changes the runtime behavior to fail fast with an exception instead of silently returning -1, preventing issues further up the call stack when reading a truncated ZIP file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test assertions to verify consistent exception throwing on truncated streams.
intent_record.what: Added test assertions to verify exception handling on subsequent reads.
intent_record.how: Validates that the stream consistently throws an exception when read after truncation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test cases to verify exception handling for corrupted ZIP file entries.
intent_record.what: Adds two new test methods to verify the behavior of ZipArchiveInputStream on corrupted stored entries.
intent_record.how: The tests deliberately corrupt a ZIP entry's size and assert that single-byte and multi-byte read operations throw an expected IOException indicating a truncated file.

grouping_result.intent: Document the bug fix for ZipArchiveInputStream in the release notes.
Throw an IOException on EOF to properly handle and signal truncated ZIP files.
Add test cases to verify exception handling for corrupted ZIP file entries.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add test cases and assertions to verify exception handling for corrupted or truncated data streams/entries.

relevance_review.reason: The group only updates the release notes to document the bug fix and does not contain any actual code changes related to the vulnerability's root cause.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by throwing an IOException instead of silently returning -1 when encountering a truncated entry, fixing the root cause.
The group adds test cases specifically designed to verify the correct handling of truncated or corrupted entries, which is the direct cause of the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line silently returns -1 when encountering EOF instead of throwing an exception or updating buffer limits, leading to infinite loops in consumers.
This condition checks for EOF and triggers the silent -1 return.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement silently returns -1 when encountering EOF instead of throwing an exception or updating buffer limits, leading to infinite loops in consumers.
This condition checks for EOF and triggers the silent -1 return without properly handling the truncated state.

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipArchiveInputStream.java b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipAr
bic_trace_step.reason: The 'readStored' method in the provided snippet contains the vulnerable construct. When 'in.read(buf.buf)' returns -1, it directly returns -1 without correctly updating or clearing the internal buffer limits (buf.lengthOfLastRead is set to -1, which later causes issues). The commit only contains whitespace and variable renaming changes, so the fix is not present.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Replaced ZipArchiveInputStream.Buffer with a ByteBuffer

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/commons/proper/com
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 29f975ea99d9b9310c6fc33ac4f844fb10f4d98a
Author: Emmanuel Bourg <ebourg@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Dec 18 23:14:26 2013 +0000

    Replaced ZipArchiveInputStream.Buffer with a ByteBuffer
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/commons/proper/compress/trunk@1552156 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipArchiveInputStream.java b/src/main/java/org/apache/commons/compress/archivers/zip/ZipArchiveInputStream.jav
bic_trace_step.reason: The 'readStored' method resets the buffer's position to 0, reads from the input stream, and if the read returns -1 (EOF), it silently returns -1 without updating 'buf.limit()'. This leaves the buffer with its previous limit and position 0, causing subsequent reads to incorrectly process stale data instead of EOF.
